name: Mineola Country Club
about: Mineola Country Club is a semi-private, 9-hole golf course established in 1932, located in the piney woods of East Texas, approximately 75 miles east of Dallas. It has been consistently named one of the Top Ten Best 9-hole courses in Texas. The facility features a new clubhouse built in 2024, which includes a fully stocked pro shop, a bar, a golf simulator, an event venue, and the restaurant Logan's Biscuit Co. The venue offers a welcoming environment for both seasoned golfers and beginners, with public access to the golf course, simulator, bar, and restaurant, while the pool is reserved for members and their guests.
why_golfers_like_it: Historic, top-rated 9-hole course in a scenic East Texas location
New, modern clubhouse with a variety of amenities including a golf simulator and full-service restaurant
Public access to golf, simulator, bar, and restaurant provides inclusive options for all visitors

food_and_drink.notes: Full-service restaurant 'Logan's Biscuit Co' open for breakfast, lunch, and dinner, featuring homemade biscuits with each meal
The Par Bar offers alcohol service; no outside alcohol allowed on premises

simulator_hardware.notes: Golf simulator available but specific hardware details not provided

faq.question: Do I need my own clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, all golfers must have their own clubs; club sharing is not permitted.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food or drink?
faq.answer: No, outside alcohol is not allowed anywhere on the premises. All alcohol must be purchased from the Pro Shop or bar. Food is available at the on-site restaurant.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The course is a 9-hole layout, so playing 18 holes would take approximately 3-4 hours depending on pace of play.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, families are welcome, and the venue offers a family-friendly environment with public access to golf and dining facilities.
